tweet_thread: mylib 1.4 ships streaming. Pass stream=True to any API call — time-to-first-token drops ~60% on long generations in our benchmarks. There's also a max_tokens budget you can set per-request or globally, which is mostly there to stop batch jobs from quietly burning money.

Breaking change to flag: client.complete() is gone in 1.4. It's been deprecated since 1.2, so if you've been ignoring the warning, now's the moment. Drop-in replacement is client.messages.create().

pip install mylib==1.4.0
https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0
drafts.hn_post:
mylib 1.4.0 is out. Two changes worth flagging for anyone using it in production.

Streaming: every API call now accepts stream=True. On long generations we measured time-to-first-token dropping by roughly 60%. If you're rendering tokens progressively in a UI, that's the bigger of the two changes.

Token budgets: max_tokens is configurable per-request or in the global config. The motivating case was batch jobs — a misbehaving prompt or a loop bug in caller code could previously rack up real spend before anyone noticed. Now it just stops at the cap.

One breaking change: client.complete() is removed. It's been deprecated since 1.2 with a warning, but the code is gone in 1.4. Replacement is client.messages.create() with the same arguments.

pip install mylib==1.4.0
https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0

reddit_draft.title: mylib 1.4 released — streaming responses and per-request token budgets
reddit_draft.body:
Shipped today. Two additions and one breaking change.

**Streaming**

Any API call accepts `stream=True` now. Time-to-first-token is about 60% faster on long generations in our benchmarks. Useful if you're piping output into a UI or anything that benefits from progressive rendering.

**Token budgets**

`max_tokens` is settable per-request or in the global config. Main use case is batch jobs where a bad prompt or a loop bug on the caller side can quietly run up the bill. Hard cap, predictable cost ceiling.

**Breaking: `client.complete()` removed**

It's been deprecated since 1.2. Swap to `client.messages.create()` — same argument shape, no other code changes needed.

Install:

```
pip install mylib==1.4.0
```

Full notes: https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0

Happy to answer questions in the comments.

drafts.newsletter_paragraph: mylib 1.4 landed this week with two practical additions worth knowing about. Every API call now supports stream=True, which cuts time-to-first-token by roughly 60% on long generations — useful for any UI that renders output as it arrives. The release also adds a max_tokens budget you can set per-request or globally, aimed at batch workloads where a runaway prompt could otherwise quietly burn through spend. One breaking change to plan for: client.complete() has been removed after being deprecated since 1.2, so if your code still calls it, switch to client.messages.create() before upgrading. Install with pip install mylib==1.4.0; full notes at https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0.

drafts.hn_post:
mylib 1.4.0 just shipped with two things worth flagging.

First, every API call now takes stream=True. The release notes report roughly 60% faster time-to-first-token on long generations, which matters less for batch and a lot for anything user-facing. The second is max_tokens, settable per-request or globally in config. Handy if you've ever had a misbehaving loop quietly eat through a budget overnight.

One breaking change: client.complete() is removed. It's been deprecated since 1.2, so if you've been ignoring the warning, now's the time. Replacement is client.messages.create(). Notes: https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0

reddit_draft.title: mylib 1.4.0: streaming + token budgets, and client.complete() is finally gone
reddit_draft.body:
Just shipped 1.4.0. Two real changes and one removal worth knowing about.

**Streaming**: pass `stream=True` to any API call. Roughly 60% faster time-to-first-token on long generations per the release notes. If you're rendering output in a UI, this is probably the one to care about.

**Token budgets**: `max_tokens` works per-request, or you can set a global cap in config. Useful for batch jobs where a runaway loop can burn through a budget before anyone notices.

**Breaking**: `client.complete()` is removed. Deprecated since 1.2, so if you've been getting the warning, time to switch. Replacement is `client.messages.create()`.

Install: `pip install mylib==1.4.0`

Notes: https://github.com/acme/mylib/releases/tag/v1.4.0

drafts.newsletter_paragraph: mylib 1.4.0 dropped this week. The headline is streaming — pass `stream=True` to any call and time-to-first-token drops by roughly 60% on long generations, which is the difference between a UI that feels responsive and one that doesn't. The other addition is `max_tokens`, settable per-request or globally, so you can cap cost before a batch job surprises you. One thing to watch if you're upgrading from anything older than 1.2: `client.complete()` is finally gone; switch to `client.messages.create()`. pip install mylib==1.4.0.
